title: Apply: adaptation and environmental change
description: Apply your understanding of adaptation and environmental change in this set of free practice questions aligned to NGSS standards.

assessment_items.question_content:
In a certain population of beetles, individuals can have either light, medium, or dark brown wings. 

At some point in time, the beetle population experienced a change in its environment. This change led to a shift in the number of individuals with different wing colors in the population.

The table below shows the percentage of beetles with light, medium, and dark brown wings both before, and multiple years after, the environmental change.

 | Before environmental change | After environmental change
- | :-: | :-:
Light brown wings | $90\%$ | $15\%$
Medium brown wings | $5\%$ | $5\%$
Dark brown wings | $5\%$ | $80\%$

**Which trait was most likely to help beetles survive and reproduce after the environmental change?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: light brown wings
medium brown wings
dark brown wings

assessment_items.question_content:
In a certain population of lizards, individuals can have either short, medium, or long hindlimbs. 

At some point in time, the lizard population experienced a change in its environment. This change led to a shift in the number of individuals with different hindlimb lengths in the population.

The table below shows the percentage of lizards with short, medium, and long hindlimbs both before, and multiple years after, the environmental change.

 | Before environmental change | After environmental change
- | :-: | :-:
Short hindlimbs | $80\%$ | $5\%$
Medium hindlimbs | $5\%$ | $5\%$
Long hindlimbs | $15\%$ | $90\%$

**Which trait was most likely to help lizards survive and reproduce after the environmental change?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: short hindlimbs
medium hindlimbs
long hindlimbs

assessment_items.question_content:
In a certain population of fish, individuals can have either large, medium, or small bodies. 

At some point in time, the fish population experienced a change in its environment. This change led to a shift in the number of individuals with different body sizes in the population.

The table below shows the percentage of fish with large, medium, or small bodies both before, and multiple years after, the environmental change.

 | Before environmental change | After environmental change
- | :-: | :-:
Large body size | $15\%$ | $90\%$
Medium body size | $5\%$ | $5\%$
Small body size | $80\%$ | $5\%$

**Which trait was most likely to help fish survive and reproduce after the environmental change?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: large body size
medium body size
small body size

assessment_items.question_content:
In a certain population of butterflies, individuals can have either white, tan, or brown wings. 

At some point in time, the butterfly population experienced a change in its environment. This change led to a shift in the number of individuals with different wing colors in the population.

The figure below shows the number of butterflies with white, tan, or brown wings both before, and multiple years after, the environmental change.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which of these environmental changes could have led to the trend shown in the figure?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.alt: A figure shows two bar graphs. The bar graph on the left is titled Before environmental change. It shows Number of butterflies on the y-axis and Butterfly color on the x-axis. There are three bars. The bar labeled White goes to about 10 butterflies. The bar labeled Tan goes to about 25 butterflies. The bar labeled Brown goes to about 95 butterflies. An arrow points from the bar graph on the left to the bar graph on the right. The bar graph on the right is titled After environmental change. It shows Number of butterflies on the y-axis and Butterfly color on the x-axis. There are three bars. The bar labeled White goes to about 90 butterflies. The bar labeled Tan goes to about 45 butterflies. The bar labeled Brown goes to about 5 butterflies.

choices.content: an increase in the number of trees with light colored bark
an increase in the number of trees with dark colored bark

assessment_items.question_content:
In a certain population of salamanders, individuals can have either yellow, brown, or black skin. 

At some point in time, the salamander population experienced a change in its environment. This change led to a shift in the number of individuals with different skin colors in the population.

The figure below shows the number of salamanders with yellow, brown, or black skin both before, and multiple years after, the environmental change.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which of these environmental changes could have led to the trend shown in the figure?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.alt: A figure shows two bar graphs. The bar graph on the left is titled Before environmental change. It shows Number of salamanders on the y-axis and Salamander color on the x-axis. There are three bars. The bar labeled Yellow goes to about 90 salamanders. The bar labeled Brown goes to about 30 salamanders. The bar labeled Black goes to about 15 salamanders. An arrow points from the bar graph on the left to the bar graph on the right. The bar graph on the right is titled After environmental change. It shows Number of salamanders on the y-axis and Salamander color on the x-axis. There are three bars. The bar labeled Yellow goes to about 15 salamanders. The bar labeled Brown goes to about 20 salamanders. The bar labeled Black goes to about 95 salamanders.

choices.content: an increase in the amount of ground covered by wet, dark-colored rocks
an increase in the amount of ground covered by  light-colored mud

assessment_items.question_content:
In a certain population of mice, individuals can have either tan, brown, or black fur. 

At some point in time, the mouse population experienced a change in its environment. This change led to a shift in the number of individuals with different fur colors in the population.

The figure below shows the number of mice with tan, brown, or black fur both before, and multiple years after, the environmental change.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which of these environmental changes could have led to the trend shown in the figure?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.alt: A figure shows two bar graphs. The bar graph on the left is titled Before environmental change. It shows Number of mice on the y-axis and Mouse color on the x-axis. There are three bars. The bar labeled Tan goes to about 15 mice. The bar labeled Brown goes to about 30 mice. The bar labeled Black goes to about 95 mice. An arrow points from the bar graph on the left to the bar graph on the right. The bar graph on the right is titled After environmental change. It shows Number of mice on the y-axis and Mouse color on the x-axis. There are three bars. The bar labeled Tan goes to about 95 mice. The bar labeled Brown goes to about 25 mice. The bar labeled Black goes to about 15 mice.

choices.content: an increase in the area covered by light-colored sand
an increase in the area covered by dark lava rock

assessment_items.question_content:
A population of grasshoppers lives in a forest habitat. Grasshoppers in the population can have either green bodies or black bodies.

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ image 2]]

One summer, a wildfire leads to burned, blackened patches in the grasshoppers’ habitat. 

**How is the grasshopper population likely to change as a result of the wildfire?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.alt: A drawing of a green grasshopper.
options.caption: *a green-bodied grasshopper*

options.alt: A drawing of a black grasshopper.
options.caption: *a black-bodied grasshopper*

choices.content: Black-bodied grasshoppers will become more common.
Green-bodied grasshoppers will become more common.

assessment_items.question_content:
In a population of plants, individuals can have either red or blue flowers. Red flowers tend to be pollinated by hummingbirds. Blue flowers tend to be pollinated by bees.

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ image 2]]

Over time, a virus causes the number of bees in the plants’ environment to go down, until there are almost no bees left.

**How is the plant population likely to change as a result of the loss of bees?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.explanation:
"[Penstemon barbartus0](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Penstemon_barbartus0.jpg)" by Kurt Stueber, [CC BY-SA 3.0](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/3.0/deed.en)

"[Penstemon perpulcher](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Penstemon_perpulcher.jpg)" by Sheri Hagwood. Bureau of Land Management, [Pubic domain](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Public_domain)

choices.content: Blue-flowered plants will become less common.
Red-flowered plants will become less common.

assessment_items.question_content:
In a population of snails, individuals can have either light or dark-colored shells. Light-colored shells reflect more sunlight. So, light shells keep snails from overheating in sunny areas. Dark-colored shells absorb more sunlight. So, dark shells keep snails from getting too cold in shadier areas.

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ image 2]]

One year, a storm causes many trees in the snail population’s habitat to fall down. This makes the snails’ habitat more sunny.

**How is the snail population likely to change as a result of the storm?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.alt: A drawing of a snail with a light-colored shell.
options.caption: *a snail with a light-colored shell*

options.alt: A drawing of a snail with a dark-colored shell.
options.caption: *a snail with a dark-colored shell*

choices.content: Light-colored snails will become more common.
Dark-colored snails will become more common.

assessment_items.question_content:
In a certain population of birds, there is variation in the beak length trait. Some birds have longer beaks, and other birds have shorter beaks.

The following graph shows how an environmental change shifted the population’s beak length trait distribution.

[[☃ image 1]]

**According to the graph, how did the environmental change affect the beak length trait in the bird population?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.alt: A line graph shows Number of birds on the y-axis and Beak length from shorter to longer along the x-axis. A solid line represents before environmental change and a dashed line represents after environmental change. The solid line begins at the bottom left corner of the graph and gradually curves up, peaks about two thirds of the way along, and steeply curves down. The dashed line begins at the bottom left corner of the graph and steeply curves up, peaks about one third of the way along, and gradually curves down.

choices.content: Birds with shorter beaks became more common.
Birds with longer beaks became more common.

assessment_items.question_content:
In a certain population of plants, there is variation in the [[☃ definition 2]] trait. Some plants have longer flowering times, and other plants have shorter flowering times.. 

The following graph shows how an environmental change shifted the population’s flowering time trait distribution.

[[☃ image 1]]

**According to the graph, how did the environmental change affect the flowering time trait in the plant population?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.definition: Flowering time is the amount of time between when a plant begins to grow and when it starts to make flowers.

options.alt: A line graph shows Number of plants on the y-axis and Flowering time from shorter to longer along the x-axis. A solid line represents before environmental change and a dashed line represents after environmental change. The solid line begins at the bottom left corner of the graph and steeply curves up, peaks about one third of the way along, and gradually curves down. The dashed line begins at the bottom left corner of the graph and gradually curves up, peaks about two thirds of the way along, and steeply curves down.

choices.content: Plants with longer flowering times became more common.
Plants with shorter flowering times became more common.

assessment_items.question_content:
In a certain population of snakes, there is variation in the body length trait. Some snakes have longer bodies, and other snakes have shorter bodies.

The following graph shows how an environmental change shifted the population’s body length trait distribution.

[[☃ image 1]]

**According to the graph, how did the environmental change affect the body length trait in the snake population?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.alt: A line graph shows Number of snakes on the y-axis and Body length from shorter to longer along the x-axis. A solid line represents before environmental change and a dashed line represents after environmental change. The solid line begins at the bottom left corner of the graph and gradually curves up, peaks about two thirds of the way along, and steeply curves down. The dashed line begins at the bottom left corner of the graph and steeply curves up, peaks about one third of the way along, and gradually curves down.

choices.content: Snakes with shorter bodies became more common.
Snakes with longer bodies became more common.
